description:
You are a localization engineer who just received yesterday’s build log.  
    Your goal is to identify every string-translation that was missing during the build
    and write a concise report for the i18n team.

    1. Source log  
       • Absolute path: /home/user/localization/build.log  
       • This file already exists. Do **not** modify it.  
       • A “missing translation” line is recognisable by the exact text fragment  
         “WARN  [i18n] Missing key '<KEY_NAME>' in locale <LOCALE_CODE>”.  
       • Example line (already present in the log):  
         2023-10-01 12:01:23 WARN  [i18n] Missing key 'WELCOME_MESSAGE' in locale es_ES

    2. Your task  
       • Use a single terminal command that employs a regular expression to  
         a) detect every missing-translation line,  
         b) extract three pieces of information in the correct order:  
            1. the full timestamp (yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss),  
            2. the two-letter language & country locale code (e.g. es_ES),  
            3. the string-key that is missing (e.g. WELCOME_MESSAGE).  
         c) re-format that information into exactly this semicolon-separated layout:  

            <timestamp>;<locale_code>;<string_key>

    3. Output  
       • Write the extracted lines, in the same order they appear in the log, to:  
         /home/user/localization/missing_translations.log  
       • The file **must contain only those lines** and nothing else (no header, blank lines, or extra spaces).

    4. Verification requirements  
       The automated test will confirm that:  
       • /home/user/localization/missing_translations.log exists.  
       • It is not empty.  
       • Every line matches the exact regex:  
         ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2};[a-z]{2}_[A-Z]{2};[A-Z0-9_]+$  
       • It contains exactly three lines (no more, no fewer) in chronological order.

    5. Hints (do not reveal commands)  
       • Typical GNU tools such as grep, sed, awk are available.  
       • A single pipeline command is sufficient.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file -->
    <file path="/home/user/localization/build.log"><![CDATA[
2023-10-01 12:01:22 INFO  [i18n] Loaded locale en_US
2023-10-01 12:01:23 WARN  [i18n] Missing key 'WELCOME_MESSAGE' in locale es_ES
2023-10-01 12:01:24 INFO  [core] Starting renderer
2023-10-01 12:01:25 WARN  [i18n] Missing key 'GOODBYE_MESSAGE' in locale fr_FR
2023-10-01 12:01:26 ERROR [db] Connection refused
2023-10-01 12:01:27 WARN  [i18n] Missing key 'LOGIN_BUTTON' in locale es_ES
    ]]></file>

    <!-- Expected artefact created by the agent -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/localization/missing_translations.log"><![CDATA[
2023-10-01 12:01:23;es_ES;WELCOME_MESSAGE
2023-10-01 12:01:25;fr_FR;GOODBYE_MESSAGE
2023-10-01 12:01:27;es_ES;LOGIN_BUTTON
    ]]></expected_file>
